metadata.title: Guidance for Industry: Patient-Reported Outcome Measures: Use in Medical Product Development to Support Labeling Claims

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must provide documented evidence of patient input during PRO instrument development and of the instrument's performance in the specific application in which it is used (population, condition).
rules.source_section: Section III (Evaluation of a PRO Instrument)
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should provide documented evidence of patient input during instrument development and of the instrument's performance in the specific application in which it is used (i.e., population, condition).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must define the role a PRO endpoint is intended to play in the clinical trial (primary, key secondary, or exploratory endpoint) so that instrument development and performance can be reviewed in context.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should define the role a PRO endpoint is intended to play in the clinical trial (i.e., a primary, key secondary, or exploratory endpoint) so that the instrument development and performance can be reviewed in the context of the intended role, and appropriate statistical methods can be planned and applied.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors planning to use a PRO instrument in support of a labeling claim must determine early in medical product development whether an adequate PRO instrument exists to assess and measure the concepts of interest.
rules.verbatim_quote: Early in medical product development, sponsors planning to use a PRO instrument in support of a labeling claim are encouraged to determine whether an adequate PRO instrument exists to assess and measure the concepts of interest.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must engage the FDA in a discussion about a new or unique PRO instrument before confirmatory clinical trial protocols are finalized.
rules.verbatim_quote: We suggest that an instrument's measurement properties be well established before enrollment begins for confirmatory clinical trials. Therefore, sponsors should begin instrument development and evaluation early in medical product development, and engage the FDA in a discussion about a new or unique PRO instrument before confirmatory clinical trial protocols are finalized.

rules.rule_text: All adverse events detected with a PRO instrument must be included in the clinical trial report.
rules.verbatim_quote: As with any clinical trial evaluating FDA-regulated medical products, all adverse events detected with a PRO instrument should be included in the clinical trial report.

rules.rule_text: Item generation for a PRO instrument must include input from the target patient population to establish items that reflect the concept of interest.
rules.verbatim_quote: Item generation should include input from the target patient population to establish the items that reflect the concept of interest and contribute to its evaluation.

rules.rule_text: Content validity documentation provided to the FDA must include all item generation techniques used, populations studied, source of items, selection/editing/reduction of items, cognitive interview summaries or transcripts, pilot testing, importance ratings, and quantitative techniques for item evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Documentation provided to the FDA to support content validity should include all item generation techniques used, including any theoretical approach; the populations studied; source of items; selection, editing, and reduction of items; cognitive interview summaries or transcripts; pilot testing; importance ratings; and quantitative techniques for item evaluation.

rules.rule_text: Items that ask patients to respond hypothetically are not recommended for clinical trials; items should ask whether the patient actually performed specific activities rather than whether the patient perceives that they could perform them.
rules.verbatim_quote: Items that ask patients to respond hypothetically may cause patients to respond on the basis of their desired condition rather than on their actual condition and therefore are not recommended for clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must consider the data collection method and all procedures and protocols associated with the instrument administration mode, including instructions to interviewers, instructions for self-administration, and instructions for supervising self-administration.
rules.source_section: Section III.D.2 (Data Collection Method and Instrument Administration Mode)
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should consider the data collection method and all procedures and protocols associated with the instrument administration mode, including instructions to interviewers, instructions for self-administration, or instructions for supervising self-administration.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must evaluate the rationale and appropriateness of the recall period for a PRO instrument, considering patient ability to validly recall the information requested.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should also evaluate the rationale and the appropriateness of the recall period for a PRO instrument. To this end, it is important to consider patient ability to validly recall the information requested.

rules.rule_text: Items with short recall periods or items that ask patients to describe their current or recent state are usually preferable; when detailed recall over a period of time is necessary, appropriate methods to enhance validity and reliability of retrospectively reported data must be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: For these reasons, items with short recall periods or items that ask patients to describe their current or recent state are usually preferable. If detailed recall of experience over a period of time is necessary, we recommend the instrument use appropriate methods and techniques for enhancing the validity and reliability of retrospectively reported data.

rules.rule_text: Content validity is specific to the population, condition, and treatment to be studied; it must be established before other measurement properties are evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Content validity is specific to the population, condition, and treatment to be studied... It is important to establish content validity before other measurement properties are evaluated.
rules.exceptions: Evidence of other types of validity (e.g., construct validity) or reliability will not overcome problems with content validity.

rules.rule_text: A PRO instrument's format used in the clinical trial must be consistent with the format used during the instrument development process.
rules.source_section: Section III.D.5 (Instrument Format, Instructions, and Training)
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important that the PRO instrument format used in the clinical trial be consistent with the format that is used during the instrument development process.

rules.rule_text: A user manual must be provided by the developer during PRO instrument development specifying how to incorporate the instrument into a clinical trial in a way that minimizes administrator burden, patient burden, missing data, and poor data quality.
rules.source_section: Section III.D.5 (Instrument Format, Instructions, and Training)
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that the user manual provided by a developer during the PRO instrument development process specify how to incorporate the instrument into a clinical trial in a way that minimizes administrator burden, patient burden, missing data, and poor data quality.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must examine all items and procedures in a pilot test of whether patients understand the items and instructions included in the PRO instrument, documenting confirmation of concepts, appropriate comprehension of response options and recall period, and adequate readability.
rules.verbatim_quote: sponsors are encouraged to examine all items and procedures in a pilot test of whether patients understand the items and instructions included in the PRO instrument. This examination should include documentation that the concepts represented in the PRO instrument's conceptual framework are confirmed, that the response options and recall period are appropriately comprehended, and that the instrument's readability is adequate for the intended population.

rules.rule_text: For PRO instruments intended for multiple cultures or languages, sponsors must provide evidence that the content validity and other measurement properties are adequately similar between all versions used in the clinical trial.
rules.verbatim_quote: we recommend that sponsors provide evidence that the content validity and other measurement properties are adequately similar between all versions used in the clinical trial.

rules.rule_text: PRO concept measurement must be stated as a specific clinical trial objective or hypothesis when the PRO measurement goal is to support labeling claims.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the PRO measurement goal is to support labeling claims, PRO concept measurement should be stated as a specific clinical trial objective or hypothesis.

rules.rule_text: The case report form in the protocol must include the exact format and version of the specific PRO instrument to be administered.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important that the case report form in the protocol include the exact format and version of the specific PRO instrument to be administered.

rules.rule_text: Open-label clinical trials are rarely adequate to support labeling claims based on PRO instruments.
rules.verbatim_quote: Open-label clinical trials, where patients and investigators are aware of assigned therapy, are rarely adequate to support labeling claims based on PRO instruments.
rules.exceptions: When blinding is not feasible (e.g., evaluation of some medical devices or administration of identifiable treatment regimens) or there is no reasonable control group, sponsors should confer with the appropriate review division.

rules.rule_text: PRO instruments administered during a clinic visit must be administered before other clinical assessments or procedures to prevent influencing patient perceptions.
rules.verbatim_quote: to prevent influencing patient perceptions, PRO instruments administered during a clinic visit should be administered before other clinical assessments or procedures.

rules.rule_text: The clinical trial protocol must describe how missing PRO data will be handled in the analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Missing data is a major challenge to the success and interpretation of any clinical trial. The clinical trial protocol should describe how missing data will be handled in the analysis.

rules.rule_text: Patients should remain in the clinical trial, even if they have discontinued treatment, and should continue to provide PRO data.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should remain in the clinical trial, even if they have discontinued treatment, and should continue to provide PRO data.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must provide the FDA with the clinical trial's Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) for review.
rules.source_section: Section V.B (Statistical Considerations for Using Multiple Endpoints)
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should provide the FDA with the clinical trial's SAP for review.

rules.rule_text: The SAP must describe the planned primary analysis in detail noting whether the endpoint will be analyzed as a continuous variable, dichotomous variable, or graded response; primary and secondary endpoints; adjustments for multiplicity; and the specific statistical methods planned.
rules.source_section: Section V.B (Statistical Considerations for Using Multiple Endpoints)
rules.verbatim_quote: The SAP should describe the planned primary analysis in detail noting whether the endpoint will be analyzed as a continuous variable (mean scores), dichotomous variable (success or failure), or some graded response; the primary and secondary endpoints; adjustments for multiplicity to control the overall Type I error rate; and the specific statistical methods planned.
cross_references: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must avoid proposing labeling claims based on statistical significance alone; sponsors must also avoid cherry picking or post hoc selective picking of PRO endpoint results for inclusion in proposed labeling.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D (Design Considerations for Multiple Endpoints)
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors also should avoid cherry picking or post hoc selective picking of PRO endpoint results for inclusion in proposed labeling.

rules.rule_text: When electronic PRO instruments are used, sponsors must ensure that FDA regulatory requirements are met for sponsor and investigator record keeping, maintenance, and access consistent with 21 CFR Part 11.
rules.source_section: Section IV.F (Specific Concerns When Using Electronic PRO Instruments)
rules.verbatim_quote: When PRO instruments are used, sponsors must ensure that FDA regulatory requirements are met for sponsor and investigator record keeping, maintenance, and access.

rules.rule_text: Electronic PRO data must be consistent with computerized systems data standards and sponsors must establish appropriate system and security controls, as well as cyber-security and system maintenance plans.
rules.source_section: Section IV.F (Specific Concerns When Using Electronic PRO Instruments)
rules.verbatim_quote: electronic PRO data should be consistent with the data standards described in that guidance. Sponsors should plan to establish appropriate system and security controls, as well as cyber-security and system maintenance plans that address how to ensure data integrity during network attacks and software updates.

rules.rule_text: The data maintained by the clinical investigator for electronic PRO instruments must include an audit trail to capture any changes made to the electronic PRO data at any point in time after it leaves the patient's electronic device.
rules.source_section: Section IV.F (Specific Concerns When Using Electronic PRO Instruments)
rules.verbatim_quote: The data maintained by the clinical investigator should include an audit trail to capture any changes made to the electronic PRO data at any point in time after it leaves the patient's electronic device.

rules.rule_text: The clinical trial protocol or a separate document must specify how the electronic PRO source data will be maintained and how the investigator will meet the regulatory requirements.
rules.source_section: Section IV.F (Specific Concerns When Using Electronic PRO Instruments)
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical trial protocol, or a separate document, should specify how the electronic PRO source data will be maintained and how the investigator will meet the regulatory requirements.

rules.rule_text: Unplanned or post hoc statistical analyses conducted after unblinding are viewed as exploratory and therefore unable to serve as the basis of a labeling claim of effectiveness.
rules.verbatim_quote: We usually view unplanned or post hoc statistical analyses conducted after unblinding as exploratory and, therefore, unable to serve as the basis of a labeling claim of effectiveness.

rules.rule_text: The SAP must address plans for how statistical analyses will handle missing data when evaluating treatment benefit and when considering patient success or patient response.
rules.source_section: Section V.D (Statistical Considerations for Patient-Level Missing Data)
rules.verbatim_quote: The SAP should address plans for how the statistical analyses will handle missing data when evaluating treatment benefit and when considering patient success or patient response.

rules.rule_text: The protocol should specify two or more sensitivity analyses with different methods for missing data imputation.
rules.source_section: Section V.D.2 (Missing Entire Domains or Entire Measurements)
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that in the protocol the sponsor propose two or more sensitivity analyses with different methods for missing data imputation.

rules.rule_text: For pediatric PRO instruments, proxy-reported outcome measures are discouraged; for patients who cannot respond for themselves, observer reports that include only those events or behaviors that can be observed must be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: We discourage proxy-reported outcome measures for this population (i.e., reports by someone who is not the patient responding as if that person were the patient). For patients who cannot respond for themselves (e.g., infant patients), we encourage observer reports that include only those events or behaviors that can be observed.

rules.rule_text: If PRO information is provided electronically to FDA, it must be placed in section 5.3.5.3 of the electronic common technical document (eCTD).
rules.source_section: Appendix (Information on a PRO Instrument Reviewed by the FDA)
rules.verbatim_quote: If the PRO information is provided electronically, it should be placed in section 5.3.5.3 of the electronic common technical document.
cross_references: ICH M2 eCTD: Electronic Common Technical Document Specification

rules.rule_text: For PRO instruments to support labeling claims, content validity documentation must include evidence from qualitative studies that items and domains are appropriate and comprehensive relative to the intended measurement concept, population, and use; testing other measurement properties will not replace or rectify problems with content validity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Content validity is the extent to which the instrument measures the concept of interest. Content validity is supported by evidence from qualitative studies that the items and domains of an instrument are appropriate and comprehensive relative to its intended measurement concept, population, and use... Evidence of other types of validity (e.g., construct validity) or reliability (e.g., consistent scores) will not overcome problems with content validity.
